title: Flashing Raccoons
description: A perspective on bouldering — terms to know, moves to learn, and how to not be a hater.

title: Fashion Journey
description: An indulgent, insightful look at a fashion journey — how it started, what it means, and where it's going.

title: The Other 3920 Hours
description: There are 8760 hours in a year. 1,920 at work. 2,920 asleep. What do you do with the other 3,920?

title: Look at the Big Picture
description: Laying every goal and venture out on a whiteboard at once reveals the connections that stay hidden when strategy is built one isolated section at a time.

title: Know Your Audience
description: Writing without a specific person in mind is writing into a void — picturing one real reader shapes every word and multiplies the impact of whatever you are trying to say.

title: Batching to Completion
description: Switching constantly between different types of tasks taxes your brain more than the tasks themselves; grouping similar work into dedicated time blocks cuts that cost and keeps you moving forward instead of spinning in place.

title: High & Lows
description: Without pausing to assess your highs and lows, it is impossible to celebrate what is working or honestly reckon with what needs to change. A case for building regular reflection into work, faith, and life.

title: Weekly One Thing
description: Identifying the single priority that would make a week feel like a success cuts through the noise of endless to-do lists and puts your energy where it actually matters.

title: Schedule Rest
description: Busyness is easy to wear as a badge, but an unbroken work rhythm quietly erodes the energy needed to do the important things well. Blocking time to rest on purpose is what keeps you from having your body force the rest on you.

title: Enjoy the Imperfect Process
description: Holding your work to a standard higher than your current ability leads to disappointment — but learning to savor the small wins along the way makes the pursuit of mastery both sustainable and worth it.

title: Say Yes by Saying No
description: Every yes is a hidden no to something else, which means indiscriminate agreement quietly drains focus from the things that matter most. Learning to say no is the clearest path to protecting what you actually want to say yes to.

title: Choose To Listen
description: The urge to give advice is often strongest exactly when the other person needs you to ask a question and stay quiet instead — a reflection on why listening takes more courage than speaking.

title: Defining Success
description: Skipping the step of defining success clearly means testing the wrong things and discovering too late that you missed the goal; this post makes the case for setting precise outcomes before any project begins.

title: Close the Loop
description: Unfinished projects do not sit idle — they accumulate as mental overhead that slowly drains your focus; formally closing them out by capturing what you learned is the only way to actually set them down.

title: Poured In to Pour Out
description: You cannot keep giving to others from a cup that is empty — without intentional rest, mentorship, and genuine friendship, the capacity to serve anyone eventually runs dry.

title: Selling a Feeling, Not a Product
description: Customers do not remember the logic of what they bought — they remember how it made them feel. Building something that lasts means designing for the emotional experience, not just the product itself.

title: Work It Out or Burnout
description: Burnout rarely arrives without warning — it builds quietly in the gap between what you need, what you have committed to, and what you actually want to do with your time.

title: Delegate, Don't Tinker
description: Podcasting revealed how easily distractions can masquerade as productivity; the real lever is identifying which tasks to hand off so you can stay focused on what only you can do.

title: Stand on the Shoulder of Giants
description: Life is too short to reinvent every wheel, and learning from people who have already walked your path is a strategic advantage hiding in plain sight. Asking for guidance takes humility, but it accelerates growth in ways that going it alone never will.

title: Work in Progress
description: Accepting that you are still being shaped — strengths, flaws, and everything in between — is not an excuse for complacency, it is the foundation for genuine peace.

title: Take Small Wins
description: Breaking a large, overwhelming project into smaller pieces is not a shortcut — it is the strategy that builds momentum and gets you to the finish line.

title: Dreams & Reality
description: Dreams provide direction and motivation, but without an honest look at reality, they stay out of reach. A healthy dose of realism does not kill your aspirations — it gives them a path forward.

title: What Keeps You Up at Night?
description: The thoughts that refuse to let you sleep at night are often pointing directly at your deepest purpose — and paying attention to them can clarify what you are actually working toward.

title: Pull Out the Root Cause
description: Fixing surface-level problems without addressing the underlying cause is like pulling a weed without its roots — it will grow back stronger. Learning to distinguish symptoms from root causes is the only path to solutions that actually last.

title: Integrating Change
description: Sustainable change — whether building a 5:30 AM workout habit or shipping a software integration — does not happen overnight; it happens through small, deliberate steps that slowly reshape the whole system.

title: Reduce Friction
description: Every domain of life — business, relationships, knowledge — moves faster when unnecessary resistance is removed. Identifying and eliminating friction is one of the highest-leverage moves toward greater effectiveness.

title: Experience + Knowledge = Wisdom
description: Knowledge without experience is a map you do not know how to use, and experience without knowledge is terrain you are wandering without a map. Wisdom comes from intentionally building both.

title: Prioritize Your Jar of Tasks
description: Drew Houston frames time management as filling a jar: rocks for high-impact work, pebbles for medium commitments, and sand for email — and the order in which you add them changes everything.

title: Decision Making with the Eisenhower Box
description: The Eisenhower Box sorts every task into four quadrants — urgent vs. important — giving you a clear framework for what to act on now, what to schedule, what to hand off, and what to cut entirely.

title: Purpose Like a Hedgehog
description: The hedgehog concept reveals that lasting purpose lives at the intersection of what you are passionate about, what you are naturally gifted at, and what creates real value for others. Missing any one of the three means trading fulfillment for frustration.

title: Live by a Compass, Not by a Map
description: Maps go obsolete the moment circumstances shift; a compass grounded in core values keeps you oriented through career changes, family transitions, and every season of life that did not appear in the original plan.

title: Your Three Business Personalities
description: Every person running a business is playing three roles at once — the visionary, the operator, and the executor — and neglecting any one of them is how things fall apart.

title: Platinum Rule > Golden Rule
description: The Golden Rule assumes everyone wants what you want — the Platinum Rule asks you to set that assumption aside and treat people according to what they actually need.

title: Who is your "Personal Board of Directors"?
description: The most important decisions in life benefit from a diverse group of trusted advisors — people who bring experience, honesty, and perspective to areas where you need it most.

title: Go for the Greenlight
description: Matthew McConaughey's memoir frames obstacles as yellow lights waiting to turn green — a mindset that transforms apparent dead ends into openings for the bold and patient.

title: Aim to Trend Upwards
description: I’ve been learning that the hardest process in life is trying to maintain balance and equilibrium in everything.

title: Balance Macro & Micro
description: When mistakes occurs, it’s easy to fret over the long-term impact our mistakes may have. But it doesn't have to be that way.

title: Kill Writer's Block
description: One of the most difficult things to do as a writer is to get over the hump of writing—the slumps that arise every so often.

title: Measurable and Immeasurable Value
description: What is the best way to quantify the results that you achieve?

title: Be a Healthy Net Giver
description: Find balance in net giving so you have enough to pour out.

title: Impact Through Mojo
description: Growing up, I always believed that in order to inspire & create impact, you had to be big or have a position of great power. But that's not true.

title: Embrace The Dip
description: Have you ever felt uncertain whether to start a venture, or in too deep with a venture to be able to quit? The Dip help's to work through that!

title: The Dichotomy of Life
description: Going back and forth, finding balance seemed impossible to achieve, and it never seemed to end. But there were moments when I achieved serendipity. When I experienced balance while facing the two opposing views.

title: Extreme Ownership
description: For me, before knowing extreme ownership, I found myself wanting to blame others when issues arise. To point at other’s faults. To not take responsibility. What I learned, though, is that extreme ownership challenges us to not blame others, have humility, and take greater ownership to change.

title: Radical Candor
description: My hope was to hear my manager's thoughts on 'being awkward through real talk'. Instead he one upped me.

title: Put In Your Reps
description: Many times, we find ourselves yearning for a specific vision to be fulfilled. There is an end goal we want to achieve. Focused on the hustle, we can find ourselves wanting to break our way into the top. But success never happens overnight.

title: Courageous Kindness
description: The impact that changes lives and captures souls requires a willingness to do what isn’t easy and to do what’s right. For me, even as I write this, I can’t help but know that talking the talk is so much easier than walking the walk.

title: What's Your Unfair Advantage?
description: An “Unfair Advantage” is anything that cannot be easily copied or bought by other stakeholders. This can be technical knowledge, unique skills, connections, patents, or a large sum donation from a distant uncle.

title: Busy is a Decision
description: I was listening to an audiobook called Tribe of Mentors, a book by Tim Ferris in which many inspirational people answered 11 questions to give insight to readers on lifelong living. One of the mentors who spoke, Debbie Millman, quoted something, that hits really hard. 'Busy is a decision.'

title: Make Decisions Easily
description: We are making decisions every single moment of our life; what food to order at a restaurant, which YouTube recommended video to watch, or what color mask (if any) to wear before heading out.

title: Design Your Life
description: When planning out your life, knowing your beliefs, your perspective, and how you approach certain aspects of life will help you assert where your true 'Your location' is,  ensuring you won’t plan your life out from the wrong starting point.

title: Infinite Mindset
description: Life is not a finite game, where a loss in a year means a loss for the rest of our life (though sometimes, it may feel that way). Rather, it is an infinite game where we are not be tied down by a single outcome in our lives and can continue to learn & grow.

title: Know Your Time
description: Whether it’s spent working on a project, exercising, leisurely watching some shows, or reflecting on the purpose of life (a timely activity I partake in), being able to know where your time goes allows you to know how you’re using your time.

title: Less is More
description: I’ve always believed that more is better. The more resources, experience, or time you have, the better you’re able to do things. That extra dollar, extra skill, and extra hour can go a long way. But sometimes, having more can be a hindrance.

title: Keep It Simple, Stupid
description: When faced with a problem, how would you solve it in the simplest manner?

title: Social Media Tools, not Addictions
description: What if I could turn my greatest enemy, into my friend?

title: Show, Don't Tell
description: It’s important to look at where our time, money, and talents are going, that way we know where our heart is truly at.

title: Dream Big, Start Small, Scale Up
description: Through what we build, what we say, and how we live, I believe each of us has the ability to inspire and change the world.

title: Priorities, Values, & Calling
description: Each season brings a new set of priorities. Recently, I started to shift my attention from my priorities to understanding my faith calling (a.k.a. values).

title: Step Out To Stand Out
description: At work, I started to turn my camera on at big company calls 📷 (as in 100+ or all-company gatherings). The reason I started to do it was because I listened to a podcast that mentioned a perspective to take in order to achieve success. "To achieve what nobody has, you must do what nobody else does."

title: Help in Humility
description: I found myself forced to do one of the things I disliked most: asking for help. When it comes to sending a message to ask for help, I struggle with formulating the right words to ask a question. Overanalysis, overthinking, and obsession over the intention of what I send stops me from moving past my discomfort of sending the question.

title: Rest Amidst Unfinished Work
description: Have you ever thought about how when we die, how many unfinished things we’ll have? 🤔 Whether it’s small things like organizing the closet, or big things like finishing a side project or hobby, in the end, we will have much-unfinished work.

title: Shedding the Image
description: When I lead, many times I feel like I need to uphold an image. Though it is fun playing around with the idea of becoming like these leaders, the problem with chasing after so many different ideals was that I easily lose myself and forget who I am.

title: Real Over Right
description: Leadership is hard. As someone who is obsessed with leadership topics, I find myself burdened by the never-ending list of expectations that I feel are implicitly expected of leaders. But people would rather follow a leader that’s real, than a leader that’s right.

title: Keep the End in Mind
description: As someone who personally struggles with leadership, whether it’s leading a bible study or making decisions in a startup, I’ve been inspired to keep the end in mind.

title: Spiritual Embodiment
description: Our spiritual lives are heavily influenced by our physical and emotional well-being.

title: Unconcious Inspiration
description: There have been many people who have inspired me through their words of encouragement. Words that motivated me to pursue coding, enter into entrepreneurship, and serve as a leader in my church.

title: 5 Reasons why you should build your own website
description: If you can code, you should consider building a website

title: Dreams and Aspirations
description: I grew up with a lot of different dreams. Each of these dreams was inspired based on the season I was going through.

title: Mapping Out Time
description: If I were to spend ideally 8 hours a day to rest, and 8 hours every weekday for my work. I would have approximately 56 hours left within...

title: Practical Intentionality
description: When productivity becomes pure autopilot, the question shifts from why to how — and recovering intentionality means slowing down enough to reconnect task execution with actual purpose and conviction.

title: YouTube Cult
description: A list of my favorite YouTubers...

title: Stretched Thin
description: An all-nighter, a storm of deadlines and ministry demands, and the quiet discovery that running on your own resolve only gets you so far. The moments of being stretched to the limit are the ones that point most clearly to where real strength comes from.

title: Storms are Temporary
description: A reminder that life can bring storms, and they are challenging, but they will end eventually. In hindsight, everything will make sense and it will be a growing experience.

title: Grateful Fatigue
description: After the workout, and a chill nights rest, I woke up feeling stiff and uncomfortable. I tried to lift my arms up to stretch, and felt the sharp sting of pain enter my right elbow. I looked down and tried to bend it, but it wouldn’t budge.

title: Blessed Mentorship
description: Playing both mentor and mentee across work, church, and entrepreneurship, Eric reflects on why intentional investment in someone else changes both people in ways that only become visible in hindsight.

title: Family Zoom Time
description: I think the biggest takeaway is that if I feel I have a stake in the conversation or great enough reason to speak up, I would.

title: Tennis Talk
description: I learned a lot about tennis rating systems from my friend, as well as the competitiveness of tennis. Essentially you’re ranked against the world, and most people who try to get into the field start as early as their teen years, skip college to compete, and only the top 100+ people can maintain a tennis career as a salary.

title: Gracious Hospitality
description: Two weeks between apartments, crashing with friends, becomes a front-row view of what generous hospitality actually looks like — and a challenge to give that same welcome to others.

title: Your Reflection is a Perceived Lie
description: Did you know, when you look in the mirror or a video call, who you see every day is a mirrored view of what everyone sees?

title: Closing a Chapter
description: After an exhausting all-night move-out, packing four years of memories from his first apartment, Eric reflects on what it means to close one chapter and carry what matters forward into the next.

title: Freedom to Learn
description: Freed from syllabi and tests, learning becomes an adventure rather than an obligation. A reminder to follow your curiosity and explore topics well outside your primary field.

title: Learning Limits
description: Saying yes to everything is really saying no to something else — learning to recognize personal limits and push back on overcommitment is how burnout gets stopped before it starts.

title: Lifelong Commitment
description: Marriage is not a contract or a romantic endpoint — it is a covenant, an imitation of the unconditional love God extended to His people even when they failed Him.

title: Accepting Imperfection
description: Binge-reading a webtoon revealed how chasing perfection is really a form of self-focus, and that accepting your own limits — asking for help, learning from mistakes — is where genuine growth actually starts.

title: Faithfulness
description: Growing out a man bun during quarantine became an unexpected lesson in faithfulness — the same small daily disciplines that keep hair healthy are what it takes to show up well at work and in life.

title: Relieving Unrelieved Stress
description: The problem is rarely the amount of stress itself — it is the stress that never gets released. Finding consistent, healthy outlets makes the difference between tension that accumulates and tension that gets processed.

title: The Power of Why
description: When metrics and comparison creep in, it is easy to lose the original reason you started something — which is why returning to your why is not optional, it is the work.

title: Stepping Out of Privilege, Out of Injustice, Into Grace
description: A bike ride past police officers became a window into the daily reality that Black friends carry, and it challenged a comfortable silence. Examining privilege and choosing to step toward justice and grace is a responsibility that does not stop at awareness.

title: How God Views Us
description: Baking from scratch — cookies, pizza dough, homemade tortillas — becomes a window into how God views His creation: with the same pride and joy a maker feels for something poured into with care.

title: Peace Amidst Transition
description: From surgery and job loss to quarantine and roommates moving out, a year of back-to-back transitions revealed one consistent source of peace: letting go of the need for control and trusting that God holds what you cannot.

title: Teaching is Learning Twice
description: Teaching is one of the fastest ways to move from knowing something in theory to truly understanding it — including the gaps you did not know you had.

title: True Expression
description: Learning to express yourself authentically means shedding the image of who you think you should be and returning to the foundation of who God made you to be.

title: Powerful Prayer
description: A prayer for lonely people — forgotten by the time the next day arrived — was answered anyway, a reminder that the power of prayer lives entirely in the One who hears it, not the one who says it.

title: Serious Silly Love
description: A season of chasing effectiveness and leadership stripped away a core part of personality: the ability to simply enjoy people without an agenda. Relearning how to be silly and present is what it actually looks like to love well.

title: Submit and Simply Trust
description: Quarantine became an unexpected space for reckoning with years of slowly distancing from God by demanding explanations before trust. Relearning childlike faith — submitting what cannot be controlled — opened the door to a relationship that had grown too complicated to feel real.

title: Breaking Out of the Shell
description: Quarantine gave Eric a clearer picture of his limits but also made him passive; this is his honest account of trying to break out of that comfort zone, starting with small acts of care toward others.

title: Grace and Sovereignty
description: Three weeks into quarantine, the pressure of uncontrollable change becomes a lesson in two things: receiving grace instead of measuring yourself against who you were before, and trusting that God is sovereign even when the season makes no sense.

title: The New Norm and Rest
description: When quarantine stripped away the busyness, a new kind of stillness settled in — one that turned out to be less of a disruption and more of an invitation to rest.

title: Unity and Learning
description: A global crisis revealed an unexpected kind of unity — and forced the kind of stillness that makes real learning possible, if you are willing to sit with the questions it raises.

title: Worship in the Storm
description: When life spirals into chaos and relief feels out of reach, the practice of worship is not an escape from the storm — it is the anchor that holds you steady inside it.

title: Balancing Energy and Trust
description: Juggling the demands of early working life means learning what drains you, protecting the time you need to recharge, and trusting that God holds the season ahead even when it feels uncertain.

title: Devotions
description: Daily devotions require a deliberate choice to block off time with God's Word — a practice that is easy to deprioritize but, when kept faithfully, produces fruit both inwardly and in how you love others.

title: Faith and Freedom
description: Believing in God is one thing — actually living in freedom from the identity He gives you is another. A reflection on what it means to move truth from the head down into the heart.

title: Calling, Identity, and Stewardship
description: A church retreat on calling clarified a core truth: each person is a finite expression of an infinite God, and faithfully stewarding the gifts, hardships, and passions He provides is how we prepare for greater responsibility.

title: Enneagrams, Knowing Self, and Sacrifice
description: Learning your Enneagram type deepens self-awareness, but it also exposes a surprising tension: the better you know yourself, the harder it becomes to choose sacrifice for others.

title: Fasting, Work, and Retreat
description: A church fast becomes an unexpected catalyst for openness — turning a moment of hunger into a conviction to share faith with coworkers and head into a weekend retreat asking bigger questions about calling.

title: 2020 Goals
description: Eric sets his 2020 aspirations — 12 books, 12 posts, 4 ventures, and 2 speaking events — while anchoring the year in a theme of Focus and Rest, prioritizing faith over accomplishment.

title: Reflection of 2019
description: A look back at the wins and hard lessons of 2019 that shaped a two-word theme to carry into the new year: Focus and Rest. Setting a guiding theme is one of the simplest and most underrated ways to direct how you live.

title: Season of Disobedience
description: How can a leader lead if their heart is not for what they follow?

title: Reflection of the El Paso Shooting
description: The news didn’t register quick enough and before I knew it, it slid swiftly by.

title: Negativity Redeemed
description: I tend to look at myself with a low regard, and expectations that seem almost disgusting. What is it about this juxtaposition? Why do I do this to myself Something is wrong with how I approach this insecurity.

title: A Rant to Stir Up a Dead Heart For God
description: How can we chase after pointless and empty dreams that lead to despair? Why do we mindlessly pursue after the many broken pieces in life that are insignificant — like grains of sand that struggle to fill the gaping hole of desire in our hearts — when there is a spring that brings about the living water of life which desires to completely engulf the darkness and fill us with peace, joy, hope, and love?

title: Well-Read Faith
description: If anyone is able to turn people’s desires towards Him through reading the words of faith by others, it can only be Him

title: Misplaced Affections
description: Have you ever felt those moments where you don’t want to exert energy to be noticed by people? To be isolated and alone. At the same time, the embracing feeling of loneliness sparks a desire to be reached out to, even with the paradox of running away?

title: What Do You Do?
description: What do you do when the world seems to go against you?

title: Do They Care?
description: Have you ever felt those moments where you don’t want to exert energy to be noticed by people? To be isolated and alone. At the same time, the embracing feeling of loneliness sparks a desire to be reached out to, even with the paradox of running away?

title: Grace-Filled Communication
description: I’ve always approached listening to people to understand where they’re coming from.

title: All about God’s Grace
description: I see my imperfection so clearly. Whether it’s maintaining friendships, finishing my work, cleaning my apartment, cooking meals for myself, following after God, or living life day by day, I see so many ways I couldn’t meet a certain standard. Things I should’ve improved on, but became after-thoughts that were never acted upon.

title: Praying for More Prayer
description: Prayer is our communication with God. As communication is important in any worldly relationship, prayer is important in our spiritual relationship with God. Many things can hurt or further grow our relationships: whether it’s having small talk, sharing a story or life update, telling about your day, fighting over a belief or value, or making amends and asking for forgiveness.

title: Guilty but Guiltless
description: Free from guilt and experiencing freedom from guilt are two different things.

title: Final Lessons & Thoughts
description: My last UTCS blog post—seven life lessons from college on journaling, saying no, building habits, community, and learning to be imperfect.

title: Going Down the Rabbit Hole
description: On chasing curiosity down unexpected paths — using Alice's rabbit hole as a metaphor for diving deep into something you can't fully see the end of.

title: Exercise it Out
description: After too many GDC all-nighters and hunchbacked computer sessions, a realization: physical health is foundational to succeeding in everything else.

title: Wholesome Hacks
description: A roundup of small but meaningful life practices — wholesome hacks for staying physically and mentally well-rounded in the middle of a demanding college schedule.

title: Interviewing on the Side
description: Walking into an interview with a full head of thoughts — how preparation, nerves, and presence shape the outcome on both sides of the table.

title: Farewell for Now: A Break from Entrepreneurship
description: A deliberate decision to step out of the entrepreneurship season — reflecting on what prompted the choice and what comes next after leaving it behind.

title: Working in Niches: Why It's Good to Know
description: Following up on broad uncertainty: why finding and working within a specific niche — rather than staying wide — builds real depth and opens unexpected doors.

title: Broad Fields of Uncertainty: It's Okay Not to Know
description: Entering college without knowing what CS even was — a reflection on how uncertainty about your path is normal, and how curiosity becomes the compass when there's no map.

title: Going Historical
description: With most required CS courses checked off, finally choosing electives freely — and why a history course opened an unexpected window into learning and campus life.

title: New Year Reflection
description: After a nonstop semester packed with meetings, projects, and entrepreneurial events — taking a breath, looking back at what mattered, and setting intentions for a more deliberate year.

title: One Desire Fast 2018
description: With all these thoughts on my mind, you can tell one of the hardest things I wrestle with during this 2-week period is knowing where my heart is at. The question I always have to ask is: What is my intention behind this fast?

title: Cling to God and Biblical Community
description: What is the connection between personal relationship with God, vulnerability, and biblical community?

title: Entrepreneurship Life Update
description: A semester recap of my winding path through Austin's startup scene—joining and leaving two startups, sitting in the in-between, and finally finding Crash Cook Off at 3 Day Startup.

title: Run Strong
description: Coming back from Thanksgiving break with nothing done and no regrets—choosing rest over grinding, then gearing up to finish the semester like a marathon.

title: Helpless Prayer
description: Do you ever feel selfish whenever you ask someone else to pray for you?

title: A Letter from Yourself to Take a Break
description: A letter written to myself (and anyone else running on empty) making the case for actually resting over Thanksgiving break instead of grinding through it.

title: Overloaded Decisions
description: Confessing my pattern of saying yes to everything—cramming courses, hackathons, orgs, recruiting—and how a summer mission trip finally taught me to aim before I fire.

title: Interview Shenanigans
description: A tour through every interview format—coding challenges, phone screens, video calls, whiteboarding—and learning to stop performing for companies and just aim to be better than yesterday.

title: Speak Now
description: Honestly, standing in front of a group of people and being able to relay ideas, thoughts, or concepts is so difficult, especially when there are so many distracting factors that come into play. Here are 7 insights that inspire me to speak publicly.

title: Learned Teaching
description: Spending two hours teaching Git at a hackathon and realizing I never truly understood it myself—a case for the Feynman technique and learning through teaching.

title: Hackathon Transformation
description: How hackathons became the gateway into entrepreneurship—shifting from 24-hour sprints to building something for the long run.

title: What About Him?
description: When I went up to Michigan in the middle of September, I was so excited! But something was wrong. Michigan holds a special place in my heart — a place where I think about friends, food, and memories. But nowhere during the time had I thought about Him. Jesus. God. The Father. The Creator.

title: Food and Swag that Matters
description: A playful reminder to look past the free t-shirts and BBQ at CS events and actually engage with what they offer—mentors, learning, interviews, and real connections.

title: Busy Reflection
description: Processing a season of busyness and the disorientation that comes with it—and why turning toward community instead of clinging to feelings is what reorients me.

title: A Story of Great Change
description: As I returned from my mission trip up in Michigan at the end of summer, I felt unsure in what the future held for my church.

title: Intentional Recruiting
description: Shifting from shotgunning resumes at every booth to recruiting with intention—researching company values, culture, and mission to find a fit worth rooting into.

title: What's Your Vision?
description: Coming back from a summer mission trip in Michigan with a new question—why do I do what I do?—and drafting my first lifetime vision statement.

title: What’s Your Story?
description: Who are you? How did you come to be here? What drives you to be you?

title: "Heart Check" Me Out God
description: The hardest struggle regarding my faith is making sure my intentions are clear — where is my heart at when I do something? Whether it’s worshipping, praying, or fellowshipping with others, I wrestle with being fully present in the moment and wholly dedicating my time to God.

title: God Works Without You
description: This past Sunday, I went to a Christian benefits concert, Helping Hands, and I didn’t experience God.

title: Only Through Him
description: This past summer, I spent time up in Michigan doing a missions trip, doing an after school program for a high school in the Southeast part of Detroit During that time, I was challenged a lot in my faith, facing faith-crises and working through many insecurities that God revealed in my life.

title: Return of the Summer
description: Back from two months of missions in Ann Arbor and Detroit, reuniting with Austin, and looking ahead to junior year with a season of investment.

title: Loneliness in Summer
description: One of the things I dislike the most is unstructured free time over break. As much as it’s relaxing and I get to sleep in for more than 8 hours a day (which I rarely get to do at school, if at all), it can be mind-numbing for me to not have something to look forward to do for the day. In fact, many times I feel very lonely during this free time.

title: The End?
description: A final retrospective on SWE with Prof. Downing: the smooth early projects, the hard group work, the Python learning, and the takeaways.

title: Finishing off Sophomore Slump
description: An end-of-sophomore-year recap—cramming Texas history, surviving OS, running 100 miles, organizing hackathons, and looking ahead to junior year.

title: Pre-Exam Life
description: EarthHack's Pick It Up project using computer vision, coding a Gmail API script for a church grad night video, and prepping for SWE and Gov exams.

title: Leaping Through the Closed Door
description: I found that my lack of experience and knowledge when initially working in JavaScript had stopped me from wanting to work with it. I realized that if I hadn’t been forced to program in JavaScript, though, I never would’ve opened myself up to working on the language in the future, if ever.

title: #ProcrastinationThoughts
description: Writing a blog post about procrastination while procrastinating on a final project—digging into why I avoid work and what urgency actually looks like when it shows up.

title: Writer's Unblock Week
description: Organizing Music Hacks, publishing a UTCS faculty profile, completing SWE Phase 3, and registering for fall classes including Korean.

title: A story about more than just bunnies and eggs
description: If I, who claim to be a Christian, am timid when it comes to sharing my testimony with people of the same faith, how in the world am I able to share the story God wrote in my life to others?

title: Hack Tech Organizer Life
description: Behind the scenes of organizing Music Hacks with Freetail Hackers—from API directories and t-shirt iterations to accidentally crashing the site on hackathon day.

title: Music Hacks
description: Implementing Flask-Whooshee for SWE search, organizing Music Hacks, and learning that alone time is sometimes the right destressor.

title: Molded Into Life
description: Building the SWE Flask API, debugging a GCP deployment issue, and reflecting on overcommitment and what actually matters.

title: Reflection
description: Realizing I'd spent over a year in CS chasing grades instead of learning—and the slow shift toward checking the heart behind the intention.

title: Arms Are Heavy
description: Algo and Gov exams, meeting Palantir, drafting a UTCS faculty article, and realizing how little code I was actually writing.

title: Back From Break
description: Catching up after Spring Break, shipping the SWE website, and navigating a paranoid game of Assassins with Freetail Hackers.

title: How to Hackathon
description: A practical walkthrough of hackathon strategy—brainstorming under pressure, building a diverse team, and making sure the presentation does justice to a sleepless night of work.

title: Who Needs A Break
description: Spring Break camping in Texas, winning the SXSW hackathon with Credit Writer, and a staycation with church friends.

title: Is This The Real Life
description: Winning Mobile Track at HackUTD with SensorStrike, planning the SWE website project team, and previewing a second short story.

title: Crazy Week
description: SWE Test 1 prep, joining FreeTail Hackers Tech Team, cooking Gordon Ramsay sliders, and studying Algo as a dynamic programming cache.

title: Hack Life
description: From hating hackathons to falling in love with them—and joining Freetail Hackers to organize one myself.

title: Free Food!!!
description: The original version of my CS event swag rant—watching students stampede a recruiter's table and reflecting on the privilege we take for granted as CS majors.

title: Fully Booked
description: Algo exam regrets over a wasted 20 minutes on a counterexample, finishing a short story without a conclusion, and wondering if there's room for everything.

title: Software Crisis
description: A mid-college crisis at the career fair—choosing a mission trip over an internship, discovering a love for teaching through proctoring, and questioning whether the industry is really where I belong.

title: Working It All Out
description: Algo problem-sets, Netflix finished early, SWE group coordination for Spring Break, and the decision to do missions instead of internships this summer.

title: Sound of my Heart
description: Church retreat debt, tedious Algo proofs, interviewing a UTCS professor on OS research, and changing a fiction story from zombies to tribal artists.

title: Not My Problem
description: Proctoring OOP and getting frustrated by lazy questions—then realizing the frustration is really with my past self, and making the case for owning what you don't know.

title: Problem Acquired
description: Liquids week of the One-Desire fast, IM basketball while running on empty, and learning to treat spec changes like real industry pressure.

title: Out of My Field
description: A pitch for CS majors to take non-CS classes—social dance, fiction writing, interpersonal communication—and how stepping outside the major builds you as a person.

title: Write On Time
description: Week one of a One-Desire meat fast, finishing Collatz after 15 hours of wrong optimization, and writing four simultaneous blogs at once.

title: New Season, New Me
description: Coming back from winter break after a brutal semester and wrestling with the comparison trap—choosing to step into a new season instead of staying bound by old insecurities.

title: Back At It Again
description: Back from Taiwan with a reset sleep schedule, starting Algorithms and Software Engineering, and proctoring OOP for the first time.

title: Rest and JavaScript
description: Five days of JavaScript30, designing a social dance app with microservices, and a spiritual fast from YouTube and social media.

title: Winter with Code
description: Studying OS with Feynman's method, unboxing a Black Friday Echo Dot, and planning side projects for winter break.

title: Stress-Free or Free-Stress?
description: Five finals-week survival tips—planned breaks, hot chocolate, honest venting, study groups, and letting it go once the exam is done.

title: Week Sixteen – Dis is Finals
description: The hardest week of semester: an ER visit from stress and anxiety, the toughest OOP exam yet, and finishing everything just barely.

title: Help! I Can Do it Myself.
description: Learning to ask for help after spending three days on a problem a coworker fixed in five minutes—and finding the balance between cheap help and expensive help.

title: Week Fourteen – Thanksgivings
description: Thanksgiving break: real sleep, teahouse visits, a new bike, and the OS project hovering in the background the whole time.

title: The Perfect Language
description: Spoiler: there isn't one. A walkthrough of languages across classes, hackathons, and jobs to show that the right language depends on what problem you're trying to solve.

title: Week Thirteen – Chill-ish Week
description: Wrapping up OS Project 3 and the OOP Life project, narrowly missing a bike at a silent auction, and mixed feelings about the semester ending.

title: What's up Doc?
description: A case for code documentation born from a frustrating hackathon with an undocumented game engine—addressing every excuse I used to have for skipping it.

title: Week Twelve – Moodify Hack
description: Winning People's Choice and Best Technical at Indigitous #Hack with a music sentiment analysis app, then getting nominated to compete globally.

title: 3 Reasons Why Python > Java
description: A lighthearted case for Python over Java after my first full Python project at the Indigitous #Hack hackathon—covering dynamic typing, the interactive interpreter, and easy library management.

title: Week Eleven - Code Withdrawal
description: Studying for the OS exam on a whiteboard, setting up VirtualBox out of code withdrawal, and the sophomore slump making itself known.

title: Week Ten - Week for the Weak
description: HackTX brainstorming, the Darwin project's big lesson: 60% design makes everything else easier, and the 50th anniversary UTCS celebration.

title: Hackathon Craze
description: My very first UTCS blog post—a rundown of why hackathons are worth the sleep deprivation, from free food and swag to learning, building, and making friends at 3 AM.

title: Week Nine - Garbage Point
description: Finishing OOP's Allocator project ahead of schedule, dereferencing a pointer I'd already seen and ignored, and Downing's best analogies yet.

title: Week Eight - Eat, Work, Blog, Sleep
description: Landing a UTCS blogger job, a 3-day OS bug hiding in a single line of C type declaration, and a rough week shadowed by a friend's passing.

title: Week Seven - Balance to an Extension
description: Balancing the OOP exam on Canvas, an OS Stack assignment full of memcpy surprises, and a constant tug-of-war between requirements and what matters more.

title: Week Six - Hard-Aware Hack
description: TAMUHack: hardware-hacking our way from an Intel Smart Glove to a virtual air drum set that won People's Choice and Best Technical awards.

title: Week Five - Hack my Life Away
description: A 4AM coding session in the lab and HackGT at Georgia Tech — building Hungry Cats over 36 sleepless hours with an undocumented Android game engine.

title: Week Four - Discovering the Lab
description: Finding the tight-knit CS lab community, finishing the first OS shell project, and starting a Netflix cache project with a partner.

title: Week Three - Bugs, Bugs, Bugs!
description: Debugging Collatz with Docker, Travis CI, and stray merge conflicts — plus dancing scheduling algorithms in front of a 439 lecture hall.

title: Week Two - Let the projects begin!
description: Learning MATLAB and LaTeX, juggling C++ and OS projects, and discovering a typing-based note-taking method that actually works.

title: Week One - Starting the Sophomore Semester (Not Slump)
description: First week of sophomore year at UT Austin: installing Docker, writing C linked lists, revamping a resume, and teaching social dance.
